# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_2.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_2.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **stratigraphic correlation diagram** (литолого-стратиграфический разрез or корреляционный разрез) — a multi-borehole lithological-stratigraphic cross-section showing side-by-side borehole/well logs correlated across a regional transect. It is not a single well log but rather a **panel diagram** correlating multiple boreholes, with correlation lines drawn between equivalent stratigraphic horizons across the boreholes. This is a standard Soviet-era format used in regional geological surveys.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **regional transect in northern Kazakhstan**, associated with the survey area described in Report 36572 (map sheets M-42 and N-42, 1:200,000 scale). The diagram appears to include **at least 6–8 boreholes/wells** (скважины), arranged horizontally across the page from left to right, likely representing a west-to-east or north-to-south transect. The individual borehole columns are drawn to depth scale and display lithological symbols throughout their lengths.

Based on partially legible header text above each borehole column, designations appear to include references to **"Буровой разрез"** (borehole section) and borehole numbers, though specific скважина (скв.) numbers are largely illegible at this scan resolution. Some header text references **"Буровой разрез по скважинам"** (borehole section through wells) followed by borehole identifiers. One central column appears to be labelled as a **"Сводный стратиграфический разрез"** (composite/summary stratigraphic section) or a type/reference section, given its isolated central position and slightly different formatting.

Depth ranges appear to extend from **surface (0 m) to several hundred metres** — possibly 600–1000+ m based on the column lengths, though exact total depths are [illegible] at this resolution.

---

## Key Content

### Borehole Column Structure
Each borehole column is drawn as a **narrow vertical strip** subdivided into lithological intervals using standard Soviet geological hatching/pattern conventions:

- **Horizontal brick-pattern hatching** — likely representing **limestone or carbonate rocks** (известняки)
- **Dotted or stippled pattern** — likely **sandstone or sandy horizons** (песчаники)
- **Diagonal or cross-hatching** — possibly **argillite, mudstone, or clay** (аргиллиты, глины)
- **Irregular or chaotic hatching** — possibly **conglomerate or breccia** (конгломерат, брекчия)
- **Blank/white intervals** — possibly **core loss, clay, or unconsolidated sediment**
- **Darker/black-filled intervals** — possibly **coal seams, carbonaceous layers, or intrusive material**

The lithological patterns are consistent and repeated across boreholes, suggesting the correlation is tracing **laterally continuous stratigraphic units**.

### Stratigraphic Labelling
To the right (and sometimes left) of each borehole column, **stratigraphic age/formation indices** are noted in the standard Soviet alphanumeric format. These appear to include symbols consistent with:
- **Paleozoic units** (Carboniferous, Devonian, or Permian — C, D, P designations are partially visible)
- Possibly **Mesozoic cover** at the top of some columns
- Formation thickness labels (in metres) appear alongside some unit boundaries

Correlation lines (straight or slightly curved diagonal lines) connect **equivalent stratigraphic boundaries** between adjacent boreholes across the panel, indicating regional continuity or offset of key horizons.

### Notable Features
- **One or two borehole columns in the central portion** appear significantly shorter than the others or are broken into separate segments, possibly representing **shallow test holes or sections with major unconformities/gaps**
- **Blank white rectangular segments** interspersed within some central columns likely represent **missing core intervals, unconsolidated material, or deliberate gaps** in the stratigraphic sequence
- The **leftmost columns** are the longest (deepest), suggesting the deepest drilling was on the western end of the transect
- The **rightmost columns** are also long, with the far-right column appearing to represent a separate borehole or reference section
- **Hatched diagonal symbols** (resembling the Soviet symbol for **crystalline basement** or **intrusive rocks**) appear near the **base of some columns**, particularly on the left side — this would indicate the boreholes reached **pre-sedimentary crystalline basement**
